label: Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting

node.label: Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting
node.definition: Weather, climate, and geospatial AI fuse gridded observations, dynamical priors, learned operators, uncertainty, and multiscale forecasting.
node.why_it_matters: This system is an application boundary where technical capability, evidence, safety controls, economics, and user workflow must succeed together.
examples: workflows combining Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting with Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins
workflows combining Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting with Images, video & spatial grids
workflows combining Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting with Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
limitations: End-to-end quality is bounded by the weakest data, model, tool, interface, policy, infrastructure, and human-oversight component. Its direct dependencies include Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins, Images, video & spatial grids.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting has an `applies` dependency on Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins with `core` strength.
